images.alt: A satirical graphic novel illustration interpreting late-stage capitalism. A glamorous, impeccably dressed woman sits confidently in the foreground, draped in extravagant couture, diamonds, and luxury accessories, calmly sipping champagne with an expression of smug indifference. Around her are exaggerated symbols of wealth, consumerism, and excess—designer shopping bags, expensive handbags, luxury watches, overflowing champagne buckets, decadent desserts, and opulent décor. Behind her stretches a sprawling dystopian megacity choked with smog, towering corporate skyscrapers, giant advertising billboards, surveillance drones, factories belching smoke, and crowded neighborhoods illustrating economic inequality. The composition uses irony and visual satire rather than realism, contrasting extreme luxury with societal collapse. Graphic novel style with bold, clean ink lines, sophisticated cel shading, rich textures, dramatic cinematic lighting, strong depth, dynamic perspective, high contrast, muted industrial colors punctuated by luxurious golds and reds, intricate environmental storytelling, expressive facial features, museum-quality comic illustration, square orientation.

Instead of Write a social media post about our new coffee blend, try this: Write a social media post about our new coffee blend. If the target audience is morning commuters, focus on speed and energy. If the target audience is weekend leisure drinkers, focus on flavor notes and relaxation. Run it twice, once for each condition. See the difference immediately. (3/4)

record.bridgyOriginalText: The problem isn't the AI. It's that your prompts are giving it one path. You need to hand it a map with forks in the road. That's conditional logic. You tell the AI: If X is true, do this. If Y is true, do that. It forces the model to make a choice based on criteria you set, which breaks it out of its default pattern. Think of it like a choose-your-own-adventure book for the AI. Instead of one predictable story, you get different outcomes based on the conditions you define. (2/4)
